mnemonics: BFST: Brute Force, Simple & Thorough. It's the first approach you try, like checking every key on a keyring.
keyPoints: Solves a problem in the most straightforward way, often by exhaustive search.
Generally has high time complexity (e.g., O(n!) for Traveling Salesman).
Easy to implement and always correct, but often inefficient for large inputs.
commonMistakes: Using it for large problem sizes where its inefficiency makes it impractical.
Confusing it with a heuristic approach; brute force examines every possibility, not an educated guess.
Over-optimizing a brute force solution instead of moving to a more efficient algorithm design.
